description_long: This airplane features a futuristic, manta ray-inspired design with a wide, sharply swept delta wing and integrated fuselage. The wings are broad and thin, with multiple fin-like protrusions and a smooth, continuous surface that blends seamlessly into the main body. The cockpit is centrally located and appears to be enclosed with a slightly raised, transparent canopy. The rear of the airplane has two pronounced engine nacelles or exhausts, and the underside shows several fin-like structures, possibly for stability or control. The surface is predominantly matte gray with subtle blue highlights, giving it a stealthy, high-tech appearance. There are no visible traditional landing gear, suggesting a specialized or unconventional landing system.
description: A sleek, manta ray-shaped airplane with a wide delta wing, central cockpit, and twin rear engine nacelles, finished in matte gray with blue accents.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide, sharply swept delta wing and central cockpit, with fin-like protrusions visible along the wing edges.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the thin profile, raised cockpit canopy, and the pronounced rear engine nacelles, as well as the fin structures on the underside.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the twin engine nacelles/exhausts and the broad, tapering wings, with the central fuselage narrowing towards the back.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the sleek, aerodynamic body, cockpit placement, and underside fins.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, sharply swept delta configuration with integrated wing-fuselage blending and multiple fin-like protrusions; no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines, rear-mounted within nacelles.
attribute_tail_design: No conventional tail; features integrated control surfaces and fin-like structures, resembling a tailless or flying wing configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: No visible landing gear; configuration not apparent.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Central, forward-mounted enclosed cockpit with a slightly raised canopy.
